reasoning: The baseline to beat is ~31.16 fatalities per 30 days, which is the average over the period from late October 2024 to late October 2025. Data indicates that Bangladesh is currently experiencing an escalation in political and mob violence in the fall of 2025. Daily homicides have hit a decade high, and political violence is at a four-year high leading into the scheduled February 2026 general elections. Election cycles in Bangladesh historically precipitate sharp increases in political violence, meaning the months strictly preceding the election (November 2025 and January 2026) are highly likely to exceed the past year's average. The closest date (Nov 2, 2025) covers mostly the already observed October 2025, which reports show has been exceptionally violent, leading to my highest confidence there. Further out, uncertainty increases due to potential government crackdowns or changes in the election timeline.
